outcome_contract.why: Guest portal personalization relies on the `?uuid=` query parameter in a fragile way that could cause data leakage or silent fallback to wrong guest data.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: All entry points to the guarded home pass uuid exclusively through the server-validated session; uuid tamper risk is eliminated; no guest can access another guest's data by manipulating the URL parameter.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: No UI-only changes expected; uuid resolution is backend-facing
Error state exists (redirect to /error) when uuid invalid/missing; no UX for "uuid tampered" case
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: No specific error state for uuid mismatch; guest gets silent redirect to /error which may be confusing
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Minimal — keep error redirect but add instrumentation

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: `prime_session` HttpOnly cookie is secure; uuid validated by format only (not cross-checked against session); Firebase RTDB reads bypass server for all booking data via SDK
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Critical: uuid can be substituted by any valid-format `occ_<13 digits>` string if Firebase rules allow open reads with API key

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: `console.warn` on guestUuid mismatch in guest-booking.ts (server-only); client-side error logger on invalid uuid
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: No audit log for uuid substitution attempts; no analytics event on uuid mismatch
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Yes — add anomaly tracking for uuid mismatch

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Session guard tests exist but use stale type (token field); useUuid has zero test coverage; uuid tamper has zero test coverage
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Major gaps in uuid resolution and tamper detection testing

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: `GuestSessionSnapshot` type has diverged from tests (stale `token` field in test mocks); `PRIME_GUEST_SESSION_KEYS` does not include `prime_guest_token` yet it is still read by digital-assistant
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Type/implementation divergence; stale localStorage key read

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: uuid resolution is synchronous localStorage read; no perf concern
No feature flag needed; change is auth-path hardening; rollback is code revert
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Hardening the auth path may break existing sessions that rely on URL-only uuid (e.g. shared links navigated directly)
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Yes — migration strategy for URL-based routing needed

open_operator_questions: What are the current Firebase RTDB security rules for the `bookings/`, `guestsDetails/`, `loans/` paths?

stage_payload.scope_summary: The prime guest portal uses a `?uuid=` URL query parameter (`occ_<13 digits>`) to identify the guest within Firebase. This parameter is read by `useUuid.ts` and used directly as a Firebase lookup key throughout the entire guarded section — without server-side cross-checking to confirm the uuid belongs to the authenticated session. The authentication layer (HttpOnly cookie `prime_session`) was hardened in TASK-05 to use a server-validated cookie, but `useUuid` still accepts the uuid from the URL and falls back to localStorage, while data hooks pass it directly to Firebase RTDB. The concern is w
goals: Determine the exact code path by which `?uuid=` is read, validated, and trusted.
Determine whether the authenticated session (`prime_session` cookie) cross-checks the uuid.
Determine what happens when the param is missing, malformed, or tampered with.
Determine whether uuid is persisted to localStorage and whether that eliminates re-read-from-URL risk.
Produce a clear threat model and recommended changes for hardening.
non_goals: Firebase RTDB security rules as configured in Firebase console (out-of-codebase-scope — not in this repo; operator must confirm rules before the plan can set tamper fix as Critical vs. advisory priority).
constraints: Cloudflare Pages Functions (edge) — no server-side React rendering in the data-fetch path.
Firebase RTDB security rules are unknown; assumed to be open or API-key-only given the FUNCTION_ONLY access model for sensitive flows (see `dataAccessModel.ts`).
assumptions: Firebase RTDB rules do not enforce per-occupant access for SDK paths at this time (based on `FUNCTION_ONLY` pattern for all sensitive flows).
The `prime_session` HttpOnly cookie is the canonical auth credential; uuid is supplemental identity carried in localStorage/URL for client routing.
key_entry_points: `apps/prime/src/app/g/page.tsx` — deep link token entry; sets localStorage session after last-name verification
`apps/prime/src/app/portal/page.tsx` — portal/guided-onboarding entry; reads from localStorage, validates session via cookie
`apps/prime/src/app/(guarded)/layout.tsx` — guarded section entry gate; validates session via cookie, does NOT check uuid
`apps/prime/src/hooks/useUuid.ts` — uuid resolution hook; reads `?uuid` from URL, falls back to localStorage; used by all data hooks
`apps/prime/functions/api/guest-booking.ts` — booking snapshot endpoint; validates token from cookie/param, cross-checks `guestUuid` against booking
`apps/prime/functions/api/find-booking.ts` — booking lookup entry; issues token for lead guest (not necessarily matched occupant)

rehearsal_issues.Issues Found: None — coverage confirmed that uuid check is absent (this is the finding, not a scope gap)
[Missing domain coverage] [Minor]: Firebase RTDB security rules not inspected (console/Firebase, not in repo)

process_topology.trigger: Guest receives a deep link `/g/<token>` (from email or `/find-my-stay` flow).
process_topology.end_condition: Guest lands on guarded home `/?uuid=<occ_id>` and the page renders personalized data.

areas.label: Deep link entry
areas.flow: 1. Guest visits `/g/<token>`. 2. `GuestEntryContent` reads `?token` from URL. 3. GET `/api/guest-session?token=<token>` validates existence and expiry in Firebase. 4. Guest submits last name via POST `/api/guest-session` with `{token, lastName}`. 5. Server checks last name against Firebase, sets `prime_session` HttpOnly cookie, returns `{bookingId, guestUuid, guestFirstName}`. 6. Client writes `bookingId`, `guestUuid`, `firstName`, `verifiedAt` to localStorage. 7. Redirects guest to `/portal`.
areas.issues: `prime_guest_token` key in localStorage is still referenced by `digital-assistant/page.tsx:107` but not written by the current POST flow (stale reference).

areas.label: Portal entry / guided onboarding
areas.flow: 1. `/portal` page runs `readGuestSession()` from localStorage. 2. Calls `validateGuestToken()` which GETs `/api/guest-session` using `prime_session` cookie. 3. If valid, shows `GuidedOnboardingFlow`. 4. On complete, calls `buildGuestHomeUrl(session)` → `/?uuid=<session.uuid>`. 5. Router replaces to guarded home with `?uuid=` in URL.
areas.issues: uuid is read from localStorage and placed back into URL by client — no server cross-check.

areas.label: Guarded layout auth gate
areas.flow: 1. `GuardedGate` calls `validateGuestToken()` (cookie-based). 2. If valid, sets `gateState='allowed'` and renders children. 3. Periodic re-validation via `useSessionValidation` at 30-min interval.
areas.issues: Gate checks session validity but NOT that uuid in URL belongs to the session.

areas.label: uuid resolution in data hooks
areas.flow: 1. All guarded-section data hooks call `useUuid()`. 2. `useUuid` reads `?uuid` from URL, validates format (`/^occ_\d{13}$/i`), falls back to `localStorage['prime_guest_uuid']` if absent from URL. 3. The validated uuid is used as the Firebase lookup key for `bookings/`, `guestsDetails/`, `loans/`, etc.
areas.issues: **uuid from URL is NOT cross-checked against the session (prime_session cookie / Firebase session token).** An attacker who is authenticated (has a valid session) can substitute another guest's `occ_<id>` in the URL to attempt to read their data.

areas.label: Find-My-Stay entry
areas.flow: 1. Guest submits surname + bookingRef. 2. GET `/api/find-booking` matches surname → returns `{redirectUrl: "/g/<token>"}`. 3. Client assigns `window.location` to the URL.
areas.issues: `find-booking` issues a token scoped to `leadOccupantId` (lead guest or first occupant), not necessarily the matched occupant. Non-lead occupants who match surname get a token scoped to the lead guest.
